methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Valar Atomics

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Alan Chang and Charles Orr, currently valued at $200M+ post-money after raising $78M to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology applies directly to this valuation. Starting from the last post-money valuation of $200M, applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields $400M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer density—while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making narrow peer benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. This range is derived from the single applicable method and represents plausible bounds around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the limited single-method support for this range and the thinness of the comparable set at this specific stage and valuation tier within nuclear energy. Any material movement in public comps or emergence of additional Series A-stage private raises in adjacent fusion or advanced fission subsectors could refine this estimate.
